note: Eight-sequence breakdown (~10–15 min each) aligned to Cameron-style momentum; IDs are stable for scene linking.

sequences.title: Judgment Day prologue / arrivals
sequences.goal: Establish the machine war, the time-travel stakes, and both terminators acquiring transport and gear.
sequences.emotional_tone: Nightmare future → cold procedural menace in the present
sequences.exit_beat: Both terminators are armed and oriented on John's world.

sequences.title: John's world / Sarah's cage
sequences.goal: Introduce John as a kid leader-in-the-making; show Sarah's credibility destroyed by psychiatry; parallel hunts tighten.
sequences.exit_beat: T-1000 closes on John at the mall; T-800 makes contact.

sequences.title: Galleria showdown / escape
sequences.goal: First proof the new terminator is unstoppable in conventional terms; John accepts protection under fire.
sequences.exit_beat: John commands the T-800 not to kill; they evade—for the moment.

sequences.title: Pescadero breakout
sequences.goal: Rescue Sarah; survive the T-1000 in close quarters; reunite the trio with conflicting agendas.
sequences.exit_beat: Sarah is out but fixated on Dyson as the 'key' to Skynet.

sequences.title: The Dyson dilemma
sequences.goal: Sarah tries assassination; John and the machine block her; pivot to evidence and coerced alliance.
sequences.exit_beat: Dyson agrees the work must be destroyed; plan moves to Cyberdyne.

sequences.title: Cyberdyne assault
sequences.goal: Enter the lab, recover future artifacts from the vault, plant charges, survive police response.
sequences.exit_beat: Building primed to burn; team extracts under heavy fire.

sequences.title: Freeway to foundry
sequences.goal: Prolonged pursuit and vehicle combat; damage the T-1000; force final arena.
sequences.exit_beat: Everyone crashes toward the industrial interior for the endgame.

sequences.title: Steel and sacrifice / coda
sequences.goal: Destroy the T-1000; eliminate remaining future tech; land the theme of learning and hope.
sequences.exit_beat: Judgment Day is not inevitable; Sarah voices reframed hope.
